content: <@586161934425128960> let's make a PR which allows same-epoch proposer preferences!

author: jtraglia
content: I'm not strongly opposed to it. Would you like to make the PR or should I?

author: jtraglia
content:
> so the trustless builder path should be as robust as possible
I totally agree btw. Let's do it.

author: nflaig
content: I can look into it, just finishing up some other stuff I am working on

author: nflaig
content: https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/5035

author: nflaig
content: I didn't touch the note in validator.md which seems fine to me, but we could relax p2p rules https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/5036

author: nflaig
content: this would be the case on gossip because the proposer itself should always have seen it's own `SignedProposerPreferences` and apply validation in that case

author: jtraglia
content: <@586161934425128960> this looks great. Everything appears to be correct to me. Nice catch on the old `preferences.slot` usage. I'm having AI double check it now. Will approve if it doesn't find anything.

author: nflaig
content: still double checking if we need to tweak some wording in few other spots, like builder.md

author: jtraglia
content: There was a valid finding. Curious which fix you prefer.

author: jtraglia
content: I'm leaning towards suggesting `if slot < state.slot` be changed to `if slot <= state.slot`

author: jtraglia
content: I don't believe we can/should do this. One edge case comes to mind. Say you're a malicious builder. You submit a bid with a value of 10 ETH but the fee recipient is an address you control, not the proposer. With this PR, nodes will forward this bid because it's the highest value and ignore other honest bids because they are lower value. The proposer will not accept this bid (because they don't get paid) and will be forced to self-build.

author: nflaig
content: I had that before but broadcasting the preference in the same slot as the proposal slot will not be valuable as it's too late, also not sure about concern you raised here https://discord.com/channels/595666850260713488/1483497365594832917/1485669424991043726

author: nflaig
content: but even in that case most/all nodes in the network should have seen the proposer preferences and would not gossip the bid + downscore the builder

author: jtraglia
content: In that case, just let other nodes (which have seen the preferences) forward valid bids.

author: jtraglia
content: Why allow nodes to forward invalid bids?

author: nflaig
content: but they wouldn't if they seen the preference

author: jtraglia
content: But your PR would allow nodes which haven't seen the preferences to forward bids.

author: jtraglia
content: What if the proposer doesn't submit any preferences?

author: jtraglia
content: Because they want to self-build.

author: nflaig
content: yeah that's true, not so convinced taht PR is good either

author: nflaig
content: also preferences are sent out so early, usually one epoch lookahead, there shouldn't be a lot of nodes in the nework that haven't seen them if the proposer broadcast them

author: nflaig
content: ah misunderstood your comment here, you meant like this https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/5035/changes/908d8847666da8f05292f145a9ed98233dddf4e0 ?

author: jtraglia
content: Yes, this is exactly what I had in mind. Thanks!

author: nflaig
content: happy to close https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/5036, I updated validator.md too now, I believe this is what Potuz meant above but I am not convinced by this change on the spec side

author: jtraglia
content: <@586161934425128960> let's keep it open until <@755590043632140352> can respond. I think there is a reason, see: https://discord.com/channels/595666850260713488/1483497365594832917/1486006101441904694

author: potuz
content: respond to what?

author: jtraglia
content: https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/5036

author: potuz
content: I think it's safe to forward bids that are sent to the withdrawal address of the validator

author: potuz
content: if there are no preferences

author: potuz
content: and if you do have the preferences then you only forward those that are compatible

author: jtraglia
content: But that's only possible for 0x01+ validators.

author: potuz
content: the attack you mention is not useful if the proposer has broadcast the preferences

author: jtraglia
content: And also, I thought staking pools disliked this. They want rewards to go into the fee recipient.

author: potuz
content: that's fine, these are really edge cases: In general it is a bad idea to add conditions that can stall the chain. The rule should be pretty simple: if you have seen a preference then just forward bids compatible with it, ignore others. If you haven't seen a preference then you can forward some bids (probably any, even allowing for those attacks)

author: jtraglia
content:
> If you haven't seen a preference then you can forward some bids (probably any, even allowing for those attacks
I still disagree. This is only making things more complex. (1) Allowing bids with arbitrary fee recipients is useless & dangerous. Using the proposer's withdrawal address is maybe okay, but it's complex. (2) Why allow gossip spam when the proposer doesn't want bids.

What about this edge case? The proposer restarts their node which causes their preferences to be sent delayed, maybe just a few slots before their proposal. Before the preferences are broadcast, a malicious builder makes an invalid bid for 10 ETH to the builder's address. What should nodes which have seen & forwarded this bid do when they receive the proposer's preferences? Should they reset their highest value bid memory?

author: nflaig
content: I am not convinced we should use withdrawal address as default, it's not a good fallback as it really only applies to solo stakers and almost nobody else
